title: mentha arvensis f. palustris
intro: Mentha arvensis f. palustris, also known as marsh pennyroyal or pale mint, is a subspecies of mint native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its pale green leaves and square stems. The medicinal properties of Mentha arvensis f. palustris lie in its essential oils, which have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include relief from nausea, headaches, and menstrual cramps, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Mentha arvensis f. palustris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical creams and ointments for skin and muscle relief.
